'''Richard Fletcher''' (born 17 May 1981) is a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a right-footed [[Goal (sport)#Rugby football|goal]]-kicking {{rlp|pr|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}} in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s.

He played at representative level for Great Britain (Academy) and [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]], and at club level in the [[Super League]] for [[Hull FC]] and the [[Castleford Tigers]], in the [[Queensland Cup]] for the [[Eastern Suburbs Tigers]],<ref name="Castleford sign forward Fletcher">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/4516717.stm|title=Castleford sign forward Fletcher|publisher=BBC|date=2005-05-05|access-date=2008-03-13}}</ref> and in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] for [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]], [[Widnes Vikings]], [[Barrow Raiders]] and the [[Dewsbury Rams]].<ref name="Statistics at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref>David Smart & Andrew Howard (1 July 2000) "Images of Sport – Castleford Rugby League – A Twentieth Century History". The History Press Ltd. {{ISBN|978-0752418957}}</ref><ref name="Castleford RLFC A to Z Player List (All Time)">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/alltime.php|title=Castleford RLFC A to Z Player List (All Time)|date=31 December 2013|access-date=1 January 2014|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120216084010/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/alltime.php|archive-date=16 February 2012}}</ref><ref name="Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=159|title=Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|date=31 December 2013|access-date=1 January 2014|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120610185322/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=159|archive-date=10 June 2012}}</ref>

==Background==
Fletcher was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], Humberside.

==Playing career==
===International honours===
Fletcher won nine caps for {{rlnt|Scotland}} between 2001 and 2009, scoring two tries. He was forced to rule himself out of the squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]] through personal reasons.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7641729.stm |title=World Cup withdrawals for Scots |work=BBC Sport |date=29 September 2008 |access-date=11 March 2026}}</ref>

===Club career===
Fletcher was transferred from the [[Eastern Suburbs Tigers]] to [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] during May 2005. He was later transferred from the Castleford to [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] during February 2007.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}
